explanation:
**Normal reference values in health** come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO, CDC). They vary by age, sex, ethnicity, and region. They are informational; your personal medical history always takes precedence.

## How it's calculated

WHO blood pressure classification.

This calculator applies the formula with the values you enter, checking typical ranges and alerting you to values outside the normal range.

## Normal reference values

| Parameter | Normal Adult Range |
|---|---|
| Blood Pressure | <120/80 mmHg |
| Resting Heart Rate | 60-100 bpm |
| Temperature | 36.1-37.2°C |
| SpO2 | 95-100% |
| Fasting Glucose | 70-100 mg/dL |
| HbA1c | <5.7% |
| Total Cholesterol | <200 mg/dL |
| LDL | <100 mg/dL |
| HDL | >40 (M) / >50 (F) |

## Final notes

This calculation is an informational reference. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a qualified professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What is considered normal blood pressure?
faq.a: Normal blood pressure is less than 120/80 mmHg.
Elevated blood pressure is 120-129/<80 mmHg — higher than normal but not yet hypertension.
Stage 1 hypertension is 130-139 mmHg systolic or 80-89 mmHg diastolic.
Stage 2 hypertension is 140 mmHg or higher systolic, or 90 mmHg or higher diastolic.
A hypertensive crisis is 180 mmHg or higher systolic, or 120 mmHg or higher diastolic — requires immediate medical attention.

faq.q: How should I measure my blood pressure correctly?
faq.a: Sit with your arm exposed at heart level. Rest for 5 minutes before measuring. Avoid caffeine and smoking for at least 30 minutes beforehand.

faq.q: What is home blood pressure monitoring?
faq.a: Take 2 readings in the morning and 2 in the evening for 5-7 days using an automated monitor. Log your results for your doctor.

faq.q: When should I see a doctor about my blood pressure?
faq.a: See a doctor if readings are consistently outside normal range, you have symptoms (headache, shortness of breath, chest pain), or you have a family history of cardiovascular or metabolic disease.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data update required
